title: RENAAL

trialDesign.fullName: Reduction of Endpoints in NIDDM with the Angiotensin II Antagonist Losartan (RENAAL)
trialDesign.population: 1513 patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy
trialDesign.duration: Mean follow-up 3.4 years (stopped early; planned 4.5 years)
trialDesign.intervention: Losartan 50 mg daily, titrated to 100 mg if needed
trialDesign.comparator: Placebo; both groups received conventional antihypertensive therapy (no ACE inhibitors)
trialDesign.primaryEndpoint: Composite: doubling serum creatinine, ESRD, or death
trialDesign.overview: Losartan reduced the primary renal composite by 16%, doubling creatinine by 25%, ESRD by 28%, and proteinuria by 35%, with renal protection exceeding blood pressure effects alone.

headings.background: Diabetic nephropathy is the leading cause of ESRD; slowing progression in type 2 diabetes had not been established before RENAAL.
headings.design: Randomized, double-blind; Losartan 50–100 mg vs placebo with conventional antihypertensives; target BP <140/90 mm Hg.
headings.population: Type 2 diabetes with nephropathy: albumin/creatinine ratio ≥300 (or proteinuria ≥0.5 g/day) and creatinine 1.3–3.0 mg/dL.
headings.intervention: Losartan 50 mg → 100 mg if trough BP above target after 4 weeks.
headings.comparator: Placebo plus open-label antihypertensives (diuretics, CCBs, beta-blockers — no ACE inhibitors).
headings.followUp: Mean 3.4 years (early stop due to external ACE-inhibitor CV evidence).

outcomes.label: Primary composite — renal
outcomes.definition: Doubling serum creatinine, ESRD, or death
outcomes.interpretation: 16% reduction (15% after BP adjustment) — exceeds BP effect alone

outcomes.label: First heart failure hospitalization
outcomes.interpretation: Significant despite neutral overall CV composite

safetyHighlights: Discontinuation for adverse events: 17.2% losartan vs 21.7% placebo
Hyperkalemia/creatinine-related discontinuation rare but slightly higher with losartan
trialSummary.clinicalBottomLine: Losartan provides global renal protection in type 2 diabetic nephropathy beyond blood pressure lowering, with meaningful ESRD delay and reduced heart failure hospitalization.

inclusionCriteria: Type 2 diabetes with nephropathy: urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio ≥300 on two occasions (or proteinuria ≥0.5 g/day)
Serum creatinine 1.3–3.0 mg/dL (men >60 kg: lower limit 1.5 mg/dL)
Target BP <140/90 mm Hg; ACE inhibitors/ARBs discontinued at screening
exclusionCriteria: MI or CABG within previous month; stroke within previous six months
ACE inhibitor or ARB use (discontinued before randomization)
keyTakeaways: Primary composite ↓16%; doubling creatinine ↓25%; ESRD ↓28%; proteinuria ↓35%.
Renal benefit significant after BP adjustment (15%) — exceeds BP effect alone.
No overall CV morbidity/mortality benefit; first HF hospitalization ↓32%.
Mean follow-up 3.4 years; losartan tolerability similar to placebo.

items.title: RENAAL — Main Points (High-Yield Overview)
items.content:
Objective and Background: Diabetic nephropathy is the leading cause of ESRD globally. RENAAL evaluated renoprotective efficacy of losartan in type 2 diabetes with overt nephropathy — data for this population were previously lacking.

Study Design: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled multinational trial (n=1,513). Losartan 50–100 mg daily vs placebo plus conventional antihypertensives (no ACE inhibitors/ARBs); target BP <140/90 mm Hg.

Primary Renal Outcomes: Primary composite (doubling creatinine, ESRD, or death) ↓16%; doubling creatinine ↓25%; ESRD ↓28%.

Secondary and Cardiovascular Outcomes: No significant overall CV morbidity/mortality composite difference; first HF hospitalization ↓32%; proteinuria ↓35% (vs increase on placebo).

Conclusion: Losartan provided global renal protection exceeding blood pressure effects alone, with tolerability similar to placebo.
keyPoints: HF hospitalization ↓32% without overall CV composite benefit
Renal protection persists after BP adjustment
items.reference: Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How was baseline nephropathy rigorously defined for patient inclusion in this trial?
items.answer: Nephropathy was defined by two occurrences of a urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio ≥300 (or protein excretion ≥0.5 g/day) alongside serum creatinine values between 1.3 and 3.0 mg/dL.
keyPoints: Nephropathy was defined by two occurrences of a urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio ≥300 (or protein excretion ≥0.5 g/day) alongside serum creatinine values between 1.3 and 3.0 mg/dL.
items.reference: RENAAL Q1: "Nephropathy was defined by two occurrences of a urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio ≥300 (or protein..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why was the trial prematurely terminated by the steering committee?
items.answer: The trial was stopped early because new external evidence suggested that ACE inhibitors (which were excluded by protocol) might effectively reduce cardiovascular events in diabetic patients with renal impairment.
keyPoints: The trial was stopped early because new external evidence suggested that ACE inhibitors (which were excluded by protocol) might effectively reduce cardiovascular events in diabetic patients with renal impairment.
items.reference: RENAAL Q2: "The trial was stopped early because new external evidence suggested that ACE inhibitors (which were ..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did the investigators ensure that the observed renal benefits were not merely an artifact of better blood pressure control?
items.answer: They added mean arterial pressure during treatment as a time-dependent covariate in the Cox regression model; after adjustment, the risk reduction remained statistically significant at 15%.
keyPoints: They added mean arterial pressure during treatment as a time-dependent covariate in the Cox regression model; after adjustment, the risk reduction remained statistically significant at 15%.
items.reference: RENAAL Q3: "They added mean arterial pressure during treatment as a time-dependent covariate in the Cox regressi..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why were patients with a recent myocardial infarction or stroke specifically excluded from enrollment?
items.answer: They were excluded to prevent acute, severe baseline cardiovascular confounders from skewing the specific assessment of renal disease progression and heart failure outcomes.
keyPoints: They were excluded to prevent acute, severe baseline cardiovascular confounders from skewing the specific assessment of renal disease progression and heart failure outcomes.
items.reference: RENAAL Q4: "They were excluded to prevent acute, severe baseline cardiovascular confounders from skewing the spe..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How was a "doubling of the serum creatinine concentration" clinically verified to prevent false positives?
items.answer: It was defined as a serum creatinine value twice the baseline, strictly requiring confirmation by a second independent measurement obtained at least four weeks after the initial spike.
keyPoints: It was defined as a serum creatinine value twice the baseline, strictly requiring confirmation by a second independent measurement obtained at least four weeks after the initial spike.
items.reference: RENAAL Q5: "It was defined as a serum creatinine value twice the baseline, strictly requiring confirmation by a ..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why was an intention-to-treat approach chosen as the primary statistical methodology?
items.answer: This approach preserves the integrity of the randomized cohorts by including data from all randomized patients from randomization through study end, regardless of subsequent treatment adherence.
keyPoints: This approach preserves the integrity of the randomized cohorts by including data from all randomized patients from randomization through study end, regardless of subsequent treatment adherence.
items.reference: RENAAL Q6: "This approach preserves the integrity of the randomized cohorts by including data from all randomize..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did the per-protocol analysis results contrast with the intention-to-treat findings?
items.answer: The per-protocol analysis demonstrated a 22% risk reduction for the primary composite endpoint versus 16% in the intention-to-treat analysis.
keyPoints: The per-protocol analysis demonstrated a 22% risk reduction for the primary composite endpoint versus 16% in the intention-to-treat analysis.
items.reference: RENAAL Q7: "The per-protocol analysis demonstrated a 22% risk reduction for the primary composite endpoint versu..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did the administration of losartan influence the trajectory of proteinuria over the 3.4-year follow-up?
items.answer: Losartan induced an average 35% reduction in proteinuria, whereas proteinuria in the placebo group tended to increase over time.
keyPoints: Losartan induced an average 35% reduction in proteinuria, whereas proteinuria in the placebo group tended to increase over time.
items.reference: RENAAL Q8: "Losartan induced an average 35% reduction in proteinuria, whereas proteinuria in the placebo group t..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why might concurrent calcium-channel antagonist therapy have theoretically antagonized the study goals, and how did losartan mitigate this?
items.answer: Calcium-channel antagonists can augment angiotensin II production; concomitant angiotensin-II-receptor blockade with losartan likely counteracted this physiological response.
keyPoints: Calcium-channel antagonists can augment angiotensin II production; concomitant angiotensin-II-receptor blockade with losartan likely counteracted this physiological response.
items.reference: RENAAL Q9: "Calcium-channel antagonists can augment angiotensin II production; concomitant angiotensin-II-recept..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How was the mathematical progression of renal disease tracked independently of clinical endpoints?
items.answer: Progression was assessed by comparing slopes of the reciprocal of serum creatinine concentration using a linear random-effects model.
keyPoints: Progression was assessed by comparing slopes of the reciprocal of serum creatinine concentration using a linear random-effects model.
items.reference: RENAAL Q10: "Progression was assessed by comparing slopes of the reciprocal of serum creatinine concentration usi..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why was an alpha spending function of the O'Brien-Fleming type utilized in the statistical analysis?
items.answer: Because the steering committee conducted interim analyses, this boundary adjusted the critical P value to 0.048 to maintain rigor of primary hypothesis testing.
keyPoints: Because the steering committee conducted interim analyses, this boundary adjusted the critical P value to 0.048 to maintain rigor of primary hypothesis testing.
items.reference: RENAAL Q11: "Because the steering committee conducted interim analyses, this boundary adjusted the critical P val..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did losartan impact the estimated decline in the glomerular filtration rate (GFR)?
items.answer: Losartan was associated with a 15.2% reduction in the estimated decline in GFR compared with placebo.
keyPoints: Losartan was associated with a 15.2% reduction in the estimated decline in GFR compared with placebo.
items.reference: RENAAL Q12: "Losartan was associated with a 15.2% reduction in the estimated decline in GFR compared with placebo..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why did the steering committee require patients previously taking ACE inhibitors to switch to alternative medications during screening?
items.answer: To isolate the specific renoprotective effects of losartan, existing therapies that already interrupted the renin-angiotensin system had to be excluded.
keyPoints: To isolate the specific renoprotective effects of losartan, existing therapies that already interrupted the renin-angiotensin system had to be excluded.
items.reference: RENAAL Q13: "To isolate the specific renoprotective effects of losartan, existing therapies that already interrup..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did the clinical toll of end-stage renal disease factor into the economic rationale for this study?
items.answer: ESRD costs in the US reached $12 billion in 1998, with diabetes accounting for ~40% of cases, making pharmacological delay of ESRD a critical economic and clinical goal.
keyPoints: ESRD costs in the US reached $12 billion in 1998, with diabetes accounting for ~40% of cases, making pharmacological delay of ESRD a critical economic and clinical goal.
items.reference: RENAAL Q14: "ESRD costs in the US reached $12 billion in 1998, with diabetes accounting for ~40% of cases, making..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why might the study have failed to demonstrate a significant difference in the composite of overall cardiovascular morbidity and mortality?
items.answer: The lack of significance likely resulted from relatively small sample size combined with strict enrollment criteria that excluded patients at high cardiovascular risk.
keyPoints: The lack of significance likely resulted from relatively small sample size combined with strict enrollment criteria that excluded patients at high cardiovascular risk.
items.reference: RENAAL Q15: "The lack of significance likely resulted from relatively small sample size combined with strict enro..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: What was the primary composite outcome measured in the RENAAL study?
options: Doubling of serum creatinine, ESRD, or death
Halving of GFR, ESRD, or hospitalization for heart failure
Death from cardiovascular causes, ESRD, or severe proteinuria
items.explanation: The primary composite was doubling of serum creatinine, end-stage renal disease, or death.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 1: The primary composite was doubling of serum creatinine, end-stage renal disease, or death. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: What was the target blood pressure goal for patients enrolled in the trial?
items.explanation: Target blood pressure was systolic <140 mm Hg and diastolic <90 mm Hg.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 2: Target blood pressure was systolic <140 mm Hg and diastolic <90 mm Hg. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: By what percentage did losartan reduce the isolated risk of end-stage renal disease (ESRD)?
items.explanation: ESRD risk was reduced by 28% with losartan.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 3: ESRD risk was reduced by 28% with losartan. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: What percentage of patients in the losartan group were titrated to the maximum 100 mg daily dose?
items.explanation: Most losartan-treated patients reached the 100 mg daily dose after titration.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 4: Most losartan-treated patients reached the 100 mg daily dose after titration. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Which cardiovascular endpoint showed a statistically significant risk reduction (32%) in the losartan group?
items.explanation: First hospitalization for heart failure was reduced by 32%.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 5: First hospitalization for heart failure was reduced by 32%. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: What was the median baseline urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio in the losartan group?
items.explanation: Median baseline albumin-to-creatinine ratio was approximately 1237.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 6: Median baseline albumin-to-creatinine ratio was approximately 1237. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: What percentage of patients were already receiving conventional antihypertensive therapy at baseline?
items.explanation: Over 93% of patients were on conventional antihypertensive therapy at baseline.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 7: Over 93% of patients were on conventional antihypertensive therapy at baseline. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Which drug class was strictly forbidden from the conventional antihypertensive treatment regimen during the trial?
items.explanation: ACE inhibitors and other ARBs were excluded; open-label agents included diuretics, CCBs, and beta-blockers.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 8: ACE inhibitors and other ARBs were excluded; open-label agents included diuretics, CCBs, and beta-blockers. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did losartan impact overall all-cause mortality during the study?
items.explanation: Approximately 20% died in both groups; no significant mortality difference.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 9: Approximately 20% died in both groups; no significant mortality difference. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Extrapolating from the data, the 28% reduction in ESRD corresponds to an average clinical delay of how many years in the need for dialysis?
items.explanation: Authors extrapolated ~2-year average delay in dialysis/transplant need.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 10: Authors extrapolated ~2-year average delay in dialysis/transplant need. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Which statistical model was used to evaluate the hazard ratio for the primary endpoint?
items.explanation: Primary analysis used Cox proportional-hazards regression.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 11: Primary analysis used Cox proportional-hazards regression. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: What was the actual mean follow-up time when the study was prematurely terminated?
items.explanation: Mean follow-up was 3.4 years (planned 4.5 years).
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 12: Mean follow-up was 3.4 years (planned 4.5 years). — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Discontinuation of the study medication due to increased serum potassium occurred in what percentage of the losartan group?
items.explanation: Hyperkalemia-related discontinuation was 1.1% in the losartan group.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 13: Hyperkalemia-related discontinuation was 1.1% in the losartan group. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: What percentage of placebo patients discontinued the study treatment over the course of the trial?
items.explanation: 53.5% of placebo patients discontinued study treatment overall.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 14: 53.5% of placebo patients discontinued study treatment overall. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: What covariate was explicitly included in the regression models to account for potential variations in global care?
items.explanation: Geographic region was included as a covariate in Cox models.
items.reference: RENAAL MCQ 15: Geographic region was included as a covariate in Cox models. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: The RENAAL study included patients diagnosed with both type 1 and type 2 diabetes.
items.explanation: RENAAL enrolled only patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 1: RENAAL enrolled only patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: The risk reduction for the primary composite endpoint remained statistically significant even after correcting for time-averaged differences in blood pressure.
items.explanation: Adjusted risk reduction was 15%, still statistically significant.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 2: Adjusted risk reduction was 15%, still statistically significant. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: Patients with a pre-existing history of clinical heart failure before enrollment were permitted to join the study.
items.explanation: Patients with clinical heart failure at baseline were excluded.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 3: Patients with clinical heart failure at baseline were excluded. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: Losartan caused a significant decrease in the overall composite rate of morbidity and mortality from cardiovascular causes compared to placebo.
items.explanation: No significant difference in the overall cardiovascular morbidity/mortality composite.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 4: No significant difference in the overall cardiovascular morbidity/mortality composite. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: The risk of doubling the serum creatinine concentration was 25% lower in the losartan group.
items.explanation: Doubling of serum creatinine was reduced by 25%.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 5: Doubling of serum creatinine was reduced by 25%. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: A lower inclusion limit of 1.5 mg/dL for serum creatinine was specifically applied to male patients weighing more than 60 kg.
items.explanation: Men >60 kg used creatinine lower limit 1.5 mg/dL.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 6: Men >60 kg used creatinine lower limit 1.5 mg/dL. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: Baseline geographic region and baseline level of proteinuria were both used as stratification factors or covariates in the primary Cox regression analysis.
items.explanation: Both region and baseline proteinuria were accounted for in analysis.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 7: Both region and baseline proteinuria were accounted for in analysis. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: Trough blood pressure levels progressively increased over the course of the 3.4-year study in the placebo group.
items.explanation: Blood pressure decreased in both groups during the study.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 8: Blood pressure decreased in both groups during the study. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: Proteinuria was quantitatively measured using the urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio from a first morning specimen.
items.explanation: Albumin-to-creatinine ratio from first morning specimen was used.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 9: Albumin-to-creatinine ratio from first morning specimen was used. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: Discontinuation of the study drug due to adverse clinical events was more frequent in the losartan group (21.7%) than in the placebo group (17.2%).
items.explanation: Adverse-event discontinuation was higher in placebo (21.7%) than losartan (17.2%).
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 10: Adverse-event discontinuation was higher in placebo (21.7%) than losartan (17.2%). — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: The steering committee members were fully aware of the treatment assignments when they unanimously voted to terminate the trial early.
items.explanation: Trial was double-blind; steering committee was not unblinded to treatment assignment.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 11: Trial was double-blind; steering committee was not unblinded to treatment assignment. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: In the analysis of nonfatal endpoints, data for patients who died were considered to have been censored.
items.explanation: Deaths were censored in nonfatal endpoint analyses.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 12: Deaths were censored in nonfatal endpoint analyses. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: Losartan lowered the rate of myocardial infarctions by 28%, and this finding was highly statistically significant (P < 0.05).
items.explanation: MI reduction (~28%) did not reach statistical significance.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 13: MI reduction (~28%) did not reach statistical significance. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: According to the study, diabetes accounted for approximately 40% of all end-stage renal disease cases in the United States between 1994 and 1998.
items.explanation: NIH data cited: diabetes ~40% of US ESRD cases in that period.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 14: NIH data cited: diabetes ~40% of US ESRD cases in that period. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: Patients enrolled in the study were permitted to take concurrent calcium-channel antagonists without protocol violation.
items.explanation: CCBs were allowed as open-label antihypertensive agents.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 15: CCBs were allowed as open-label antihypertensive agents. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: The intention-to-treat approach excluded data from patients the moment they permanently discontinued their study medication.
items.explanation: ITT included patients from randomization to study end regardless of adherence.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 16: ITT included patients from randomization to study end regardless of adherence. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: Baseline pulse pressures were not significantly different between the losartan and placebo groups.
items.explanation: Baseline characteristics including pulse pressure were well balanced.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 17: Baseline characteristics including pulse pressure were well balanced. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: Losartan reduced the rate of decline in renal function as assessed by the slopes of the reciprocal serum creatinine concentration.
items.explanation: Reciprocal creatinine slopes favored losartan.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 18: Reciprocal creatinine slopes favored losartan. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: The HOPE study, cited by the authors, previously proved that ACE inhibitors significantly decrease hospitalizations for heart failure in diabetic patients.
items.explanation: HOPE showed ACE benefit on heart failure signs but not significant HF hospitalization reduction; RENAAL showed ARB reduced HF hospitalization.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 19: HOPE showed ACE benefit on heart failure signs but not significant HF hospitalization reduction; RENAAL showed ARB reduced HF hospitalization. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.statement: The risk of the combined secondary endpoint of ESRD or death was reduced by 20% in the losartan group.
items.explanation: ESRD-or-death secondary endpoint showed ~20% risk reduction.
items.reference: RENAAL T/F 20: ESRD-or-death secondary endpoint showed ~20% risk reduction. — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.
